  • Hide All Code

Validation: fixated vs. analyzeeyedry on C1L1.asc

Auto-generated validation report

2026-04-08

1. Introduction

This document validates the fixated R package by comparing its eye-movement measure computations against the analyzeeyedry R package, which implements the exact algorithms from the classic eyedry program (Clifton, 1990) as used with the UMass EyeTrack software.

The test data is C1L1.asc, a single-subject EyeLink ASC file recorded with the UMass EyeTrack 6 software. The file contains Spanish reading data.

Key format differences in C1L1.asc versus a standard EyeLink file

Feature C1L1.asc (UMass EyeTrack) Standard EyeLink/OpenSesame
Trial ID format TRIALID P100I8D0 ([prefix][cond]I[item]D[n]) TRIAL 1 ITEM 8 WORD 1 …
Region messages REGION CHAR <idx> <line> <char> <xl> <yt> <xr> <yb> TRIAL … WORD … RIGHT_BOUNDARY …
Display onset SYNCTIME (no payload; timestamp is the onset) DISPLAY ON / t_display_on in trial_db
Trial end TRIAL_RESULT <code> stop_trial MSG
Non-ASCII chars Replaced by spaces in character list Full Unicode (EDF → ASC)
Eye recorded Right eye only Configurable

Analysis regions

Both algorithms treat every word as one analysis region. Word boundaries are derived from the REGION CHAR messages: a new word starts at the first character of the sentence and at every non-space character that immediately follows a space.

2. Setup

# Install packages from local source if not already available
# Paths are relative to the Rmd's working directory (validation/)
pkg_root       <- file.path(getwd(), "..")          # fixated package root
analyzeeyedry_dir <- file.path(getwd(), "analyzeeyedry")

if (!requireNamespace("analyzeeyedry", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages(analyzeeyedry_dir, repos = NULL, type = "source")
}

devtools::load_all(pkg_root)  # Load fixated functions without installing
if (!requireNamespace("fixated", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages(pkg_root, repos = NULL, type = "source")
}

library(analyzeeyedry)
library(fixated)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(knitr)

ASC_FILE <- file.path(pkg_root, "data_to_incorporate", "C1L1.asc")
stopifnot(file.exists(ASC_FILE))
cat("ASC file:", ASC_FILE, "\n")
## ASC file: /home/rstudio/projects/fixated/validation/../data_to_incorporate/C1L1.asc

3. Custom parsing helpers for C1L1.asc

Because C1L1.asc uses the UMass EyeTrack message format, neither package reads it “out of the box” without configuration. The helper functions below extract all needed data directly from the file and create a tidy trial_list that both analyses can work from.

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Low-level parser: read C1L1.asc into a list of trial objects.
# Each trial contains:
#   $trial_id, $prefix, $cond, $item, $seq
#   $synctime   – absolute timestamp of SYNCTIME (display onset)
#   $chars      – data.frame(idx, char, x_left, y_top, x_right, y_bottom)
#   $fixations  – data.frame(t_start, t_end, duration, x, y)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
parse_c1l1 <- function(filepath, trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]") {
  lines <- readLines(filepath, warn = FALSE)
  
  trial_list <- list()
  in_trial   <- FALSE
  seq_n      <- 0L
  
  # per-trial accumulators
  cond <- NA_integer_; item <- NA_integer_; tid <- ""
  prefix <- ""; synctime <- NA_real_
  chars_list <- list(); fix_list <- list()
  
  for (line in lines) {
    # ---- TRIALID ----
    if (grepl("MSG\\s.*TRIALID\\s+(\\S+)", line)) {
      m_tid <- regmatches(line, regexpr("TRIALID\\s+(\\S+)", line))
      if (length(m_tid) > 0L) {
        trial_id <- sub("TRIALID\\s+", "", m_tid)
        if (grepl(trial_pattern, trial_id)) {
          m <- regmatches(trial_id, regexpr("^([A-Za-z]+)(\\d+)[A-Za-z](\\d+)", trial_id))
          if (length(m) > 0L) {
            nums <- regmatches(m, gregexpr("\\d+", m))[[1L]]
            pfx  <- regmatches(m, gregexpr("[A-Za-z]+", m))[[1L]][1L]
            if (length(nums) >= 2L) {
              seq_n      <- seq_n + 1L
              cond       <- as.integer(nums[1L])
              item       <- as.integer(nums[2L])
              tid        <- trial_id
              prefix     <- pfx
              synctime   <- NA_real_
              chars_list <- list()
              fix_list   <- list()
              in_trial   <- TRUE
            }
          }
        } else {
          in_trial <- FALSE
        }
      }
      next
    }
    
    if (!in_trial) next
    
    # ---- REGION CHAR ----
    if (grepl("REGION CHAR", line)) {
      parts <- strsplit(trimws(line), "\\s+")[[1L]]
      np    <- length(parts)
      if (np >= 10L) {
        idx   <- as.integer(parts[5L])
        ch    <- if (np >= 11L) parts[7L] else " "
        x_l   <- as.numeric(parts[np - 3L])
        y_t   <- as.numeric(parts[np - 2L])
        x_r   <- as.numeric(parts[np - 1L])
        y_b   <- as.numeric(parts[np])
        chars_list[[length(chars_list) + 1L]] <-
          data.frame(idx = idx, char = ch,
                     x_left = x_l, y_top = y_t,
                     x_right = x_r, y_bottom = y_b,
                     stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
      }
      next
    }
    
    # ---- SYNCTIME ----
    if (grepl("SYNCTIME", line)) {
      # Format: MSG <timestamp> SYNCTIME
      parts <- strsplit(trimws(line), "\\s+")[[1L]]
      if (length(parts) >= 2L)
        synctime <- as.numeric(parts[2L])
      next
    }
    
    # ---- EFIX R ----
    if (grepl("^EFIX\\s+R", line)) {
      parts <- strsplit(trimws(line), "\\s+")[[1L]]
      # EFIX R t_start t_end duration x y pupil
      if (length(parts) >= 7L) {
        fix_list[[length(fix_list) + 1L]] <- data.frame(
          t_start  = as.numeric(parts[3L]),
          t_end    = as.numeric(parts[4L]),
          duration = as.numeric(parts[5L]),
          x        = as.numeric(parts[6L]),
          y        = as.numeric(parts[7L]),
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
      }
      next
    }
    
    # ---- TRIAL_RESULT ----
    if (grepl("TRIAL_RESULT", line)) {
      if (in_trial && length(chars_list) > 0L) {
        chars_df <- do.call(rbind, chars_list)
        chars_df <- chars_df[order(chars_df$idx), ]
        fix_df   <- if (length(fix_list) > 0L)
                      do.call(rbind, fix_list)
                    else
                      data.frame(t_start = numeric(), t_end = numeric(),
                                 duration = numeric(), x = numeric(), y = numeric())
        trial_list[[length(trial_list) + 1L]] <- list(
          trial_id = tid, prefix = prefix,
          cond = cond, item = item, seq = seq_n,
          synctime = synctime,
          chars    = chars_df,
          fixations = fix_df
        )
      }
      in_trial <- FALSE
      next
    }
  }
  trial_list
}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build pixel-space word ROIs from the chars data frame.
# Word starts are determined by the space-detection rule (same as analyzeeyedry).
# The space between two words is included in the PRECEDING word's x_end
# (matching analyzeeyedry's region convention).
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
build_word_rois <- function(chars_df) {
  n <- nrow(chars_df)
  if (n == 0L) return(data.frame())
  
  # Detect word starts: first char, and any non-space after a space
  word_start_char_idx <- integer(0)
  for (ci in seq_len(n)) {
    if (ci == 1L) {
      word_start_char_idx <- c(word_start_char_idx, ci)
    } else if (chars_df$char[ci - 1L] == " " && chars_df$char[ci] != " ") {
      word_start_char_idx <- c(word_start_char_idx, ci)
    }
  }
  
  nw <- length(word_start_char_idx)
  if (nw == 0L) return(data.frame())
  
  # Word end (in the chars_df row index) = one before next word start
  word_end_char_idx <- c(word_start_char_idx[-1L] - 1L, n)
  
  rois <- vector("list", nw)
  for (w in seq_len(nw)) {
    rows   <- word_start_char_idx[w]:word_end_char_idx[w]
    x_s    <- chars_df$x_left[word_start_char_idx[w]]
    x_e    <- chars_df$x_right[word_end_char_idx[w]]
    y_s    <- min(chars_df$y_top[rows])
    y_e    <- max(chars_df$y_bottom[rows])
    # Reconstruct word text (spaces are either literal or non-ASCII placeholders)
    word_str <- paste(chars_df$char[word_start_char_idx[w]:
                                      word_end_char_idx[w]], collapse = "")
    rois[[w]] <- data.frame(
      word_id = w, word = word_str,
      x_start = x_s, x_end = x_e,
      y_start = y_s, y_end = y_e,
      char_start_idx = chars_df$idx[word_start_char_idx[w]],
      char_end_idx   = chars_df$idx[word_end_char_idx[w]],
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }
  do.call(rbind, rois)
}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Parse the whole file and attach word ROIs to every trial.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
trial_list <- parse_c1l1(ASC_FILE)
for (ti in seq_along(trial_list)) {
  trial_list[[ti]]$word_rois <- build_word_rois(trial_list[[ti]]$chars)
}

cat(sprintf("Parsed %d trials\n", length(trial_list)))
## Parsed 175 trials
prefix_table <- table(sapply(trial_list, `[[`, "prefix"))
print(prefix_table)
## 
##   E   F   P 
## 130  34  11

4. analyzeeyedry analysis

analyzeeyedry converts pixel coordinates to 0-indexed character positions using the REGION CHAR messages and expresses fixation times relative to SYNCTIME. The package-level functions asc_to_fixations() and asc_to_cnt() do this automatically.

# Build word regions (char-index space) from the ASC file
word_regions_edry <- asc_to_cnt(
  ASC_FILE,
  trial_pattern  = "^[A-Za-z]",
  spillover_gap  = 1L,
  spillover_size = 20L
)
cat(sprintf("Word regions: %d trial × cond combinations\n", nrow(word_regions_edry)))
## Word regions: 175 trial × cond combinations
cat(sprintf("Max regions per trial: %d\n",
            max(word_regions_edry$nreg, na.rm = TRUE)))
## Max regions per trial: 23
# Extract character-indexed fixations
fix_edry <- asc_to_fixations(
  ASC_FILE,
  subj          = 1L,
  trial_pattern = "^[A-Za-z]"
)
cat(sprintf("Fixations extracted: %d\n", nrow(fix_edry)))
## Fixations extracted: 2572
# Assign fixations to word regions
fix_edry_r <- assign_regions_to_fixations(fix_edry, word_regions_edry)

# Compute all eyedry measures
# shorttime = 80 ms: ignore fixations <= 80 ms
# longtime  = 800 ms: discard region if a fixation > 800 ms is encountered
nregmax_val <- max(word_regions_edry$nreg, na.rm = TRUE)
measures_edry <- compute_fixation_measures(
  fix_edry_r,
  word_regions_edry,
  shorttime = 80L,
  longtime  = 800L,
  nregmax   = nregmax_val
)
cat(sprintf("Measures computed for %d trials\n", nrow(measures_edry)))
## Measures computed for 175 trials
head(measures_edry[, 1:14])

5. fixated analysis

fixated::compute_eye_measures() works in pixel coordinates. We feed it the fixations and word ROIs derived from the same REGION CHAR messages.

Two additional alignment decisions must be made explicit:

  1. Display-onset filter: analyzeeyedry skips fixations that end before SYNCTIME and truncates fixations that straddle the onset (setting the relative start time to 0). fixated with a trial_db filter uses start_time > t_display_on (strict), which excludes straddling fixations. We test both approaches and discuss the difference.

  2. Duration filtering: analyzeeyedry skips fixations ≤ 80 ms and discards regions when a fixation > 800 ms occurs. fixated has no built-in duration cutoffs. We show results with and without matching the eyedry cutoffs applied upstream.

# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build a tidy fixations tibble and a tidy ROI tibble for fixated,
# applying the SYNCTIME (display-onset) filter to mirror analyzeeyedry.
# -------------------------------------------------------------------------
build_fixated_inputs <- function(tl,
                                 onset_filter = c("synctime_strict",
                                                  "synctime_truncate",
                                                  "none")) {
  onset_filter <- match.arg(onset_filter)
  
  fix_rows <- list()
  roi_rows <- list()
  
  for (ti in seq_along(tl)) {
    tr     <- tl[[ti]]
    trial_nr <- ti
    cond_v   <- tr$cond
    item_v   <- tr$item
    fix_df   <- tr$fixations
    rois     <- tr$word_rois
    
    if (nrow(fix_df) == 0L || nrow(rois) == 0L) next
    
    synctime_val <- tr$synctime
    
    # Apply onset filter
    if (onset_filter == "synctime_strict" && !is.na(synctime_val)) {
      # Include only fixations that STARTED after display onset
      fix_df <- fix_df[fix_df$t_start > synctime_val, , drop = FALSE]
    } else if (onset_filter == "synctime_truncate" && !is.na(synctime_val)) {
      # Truncate fixations that straddle onset (matching analyzeeyedry)
      fix_df <- fix_df[fix_df$t_end > synctime_val, , drop = FALSE]
      straddle <- !is.na(fix_df$t_start) & fix_df$t_start <= synctime_val
      if (any(straddle)) {
        # Truncate: adjust start_time and recalculate duration
        fix_df$t_start[straddle]  <- synctime_val
        fix_df$duration[straddle] <- fix_df$t_end[straddle] -
                                     fix_df$t_start[straddle]
      }
    }
    
    if (nrow(fix_df) == 0L) next
    
    # Build fixation rows
    for (fi in seq_len(nrow(fix_df))) {
      fix_rows[[length(fix_rows) + 1L]] <- data.frame(
        trial_nr   = trial_nr,
        cond       = cond_v,
        item       = item_v,
        start_time = fix_df$t_start[fi],
        end_time   = fix_df$t_end[fi],
        duration   = fix_df$duration[fi],
        avg_x      = fix_df$x[fi],
        avg_y      = fix_df$y[fi],
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
    }
    
    # Build ROI rows
    rois$trial_nr <- trial_nr
    rois$cond     <- cond_v
    rois$item     <- item_v
    roi_rows[[length(roi_rows) + 1L]] <- rois
  }
  
  fix_tbl <- if (length(fix_rows) > 0L)
               dplyr::as_tibble(do.call(rbind, fix_rows))
             else
               dplyr::tibble()
  roi_tbl <- if (length(roi_rows) > 0L)
               dplyr::as_tibble(do.call(rbind, roi_rows))
             else
               dplyr::tibble()
  
  list(fixations = fix_tbl, roi = roi_tbl)
}


# ---- Version A: synctime_strict (start_time > SYNCTIME) ----
inp_strict <- build_fixated_inputs(trial_list, onset_filter = "synctime_strict")
cat("Fixated (strict SYNCTIME filter):",
    nrow(inp_strict$fixations), "fixations across",
    n_distinct(inp_strict$fixations$trial_nr), "trials\n")
## Fixated (strict SYNCTIME filter): 2415 fixations across 175 trials
measures_fix_strict <- compute_eye_measures(
  inp_strict$fixations,
  inp_strict$roi,
  trial_col = "trial_nr"
)

# ---- Version B: synctime_truncate (mirrors analyzeeyedry) ----
inp_trunc <- build_fixated_inputs(trial_list, onset_filter = "synctime_truncate")
cat("Fixated (truncate SYNCTIME filter):",
    nrow(inp_trunc$fixations), "fixations across",
    n_distinct(inp_trunc$fixations$trial_nr), "trials\n")
## Fixated (truncate SYNCTIME filter): 2572 fixations across 175 trials
measures_fix_trunc <- compute_eye_measures(
  inp_trunc$fixations,
  inp_trunc$roi,
  trial_col = "trial_nr"
)

6. Linking the two output formats

analyzeeyedry produces a wide table (one row per trial, one column per region × measure). fixated produces a long table (one row per trial × word). We convert analyzeeyedry’s output to long format for comparison.

# Map (cond, item) → seq/trial_nr via the parse_c1l1 result
trial_key <- dplyr::tibble(
  seq      = sapply(trial_list, `[[`, "seq"),
  trial_nr = seq_along(trial_list),
  cond     = sapply(trial_list, `[[`, "cond"),
  item     = sapply(trial_list, `[[`, "item")
)

# Pivot analyzeeyedry wide → long
#   cols: ff_R1…ff_Rn, fp_R1…fp_Rn, tt_R1…tt_Rn, gp_R1…gp_Rn
#   map:  ff → ffd,  fp → gd,  tt → tvt,  gp → gpt
pivot_edry_long <- function(wide_df, trial_key) {
  # Identify measure columns
  meas_cols <- names(wide_df)[grepl("^(ff|fp|tt|gp)_R\\d+$", names(wide_df))]
  
  long <- wide_df %>%
    tidyr::pivot_longer(
      cols     = dplyr::all_of(meas_cols),
      names_to = c("measure", "word_id"),
      names_pattern = "^(.+)_R(\\d+)$",
      values_to = "value"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      word_id = as.integer(word_id),
      measure = dplyr::recode(measure,
                              ff = "ffd", fp = "gd", tt = "tvt", gp = "gpt")
    ) %>%
    tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = measure, values_from = value)
  
  # Join trial_nr
  long <- long %>%
    dplyr::left_join(trial_key, by = c("cond", "item"))
  
  long
}

edry_long <- pivot_edry_long(measures_edry, trial_key)
cat("analyzeeyedry long format:", nrow(edry_long), "rows\n")
## analyzeeyedry long format: 4025 rows
# Merge analyzeeyedry and fixated (truncate version) results for comparison
compare_df <- edry_long %>%
  dplyr::select(trial_nr, cond, item, word_id, 
                ffd_edry = ffd, gd_edry = gd, gpt_edry = gpt, tvt_edry = tvt) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    measures_fix_trunc %>%
      dplyr::select(trial_nr, word_id,
                    ffd_fix = ffd, gd_fix = gd, gpt_fix = gpt, tvt_fix = tvt),
    by = c("trial_nr", "word_id")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(trial_nr, word_id)

cat(sprintf(
  "Comparison table: %d rows (%d trial × word combinations)\n",
  nrow(compare_df), nrow(compare_df)
))
## Comparison table: 2037 rows (2037 trial × word combinations)

7. Comparison

7.1 Gaze Duration (GD / fp)

GD in fixated equals “gaze duration” and corresponds to fp (first-pass) in analyzeeyedry.

gd_comp <- compare_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(gd_edry) | !is.na(gd_fix)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    gd_diff     = gd_fix - gd_edry,
    gd_agree    = is.na(gd_fix) == is.na(gd_edry) &
                  (is.na(gd_diff) | abs(gd_diff) < 1),
    status      = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(gd_edry) & is.na(gd_fix) ~ "both_NA",
      is.na(gd_edry) & !is.na(gd_fix) ~ "only_fixated",
      !is.na(gd_edry) & is.na(gd_fix) ~ "only_eyedry",
      abs(gd_diff) < 1                 ~ "agree",
      TRUE                             ~ "disagree"
    )
  )

cat("GD agreement summary:\n")
## GD agreement summary:
print(table(gd_comp$status))
## 
##        agree     disagree  only_eyedry only_fixated 
##           75         1144           34           27
ggplot(gd_comp %>% dplyr::filter(status == "agree" | status == "disagree"),
       aes(x = gd_edry, y = gd_fix, colour = status)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Gaze Duration: analyzeeyedry vs fixated",
       x = "analyzeeyedry fp (ms)", y = "fixated gd (ms)") +
  theme_bw()

gd_bad <- gd_comp %>%
  dplyr::filter(status %in% c("only_fixated", "only_eyedry", "disagree")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(abs(gd_diff))
cat(sprintf("%d GD discrepancies found\n", nrow(gd_bad)))
## 1205 GD discrepancies found
if (nrow(gd_bad) > 0) knitr::kable(head(gd_bad, 30))

7.2 Total Viewing Time (TVT / tt)

tvt_comp <- compare_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(tvt_edry) | !is.na(tvt_fix)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    tvt_diff  = tvt_fix - tvt_edry,
    status    = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(tvt_edry) & is.na(tvt_fix)  ~ "both_NA",
      is.na(tvt_edry) & !is.na(tvt_fix) ~ "only_fixated",
      !is.na(tvt_edry) & is.na(tvt_fix) ~ "only_eyedry",
      abs(tvt_diff) < 1                  ~ "agree",
      TRUE                               ~ "disagree"
    )
  )

cat("TVT agreement summary:\n")
## TVT agreement summary:
print(table(tvt_comp$status))
## 
##        agree     disagree  only_eyedry only_fixated 
##           62         1463           11           19
ggplot(tvt_comp %>% dplyr::filter(status %in% c("agree", "disagree")),
       aes(x = tvt_edry, y = tvt_fix, colour = status)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "Total Viewing Time: analyzeeyedry vs fixated",
       x = "analyzeeyedry tt (ms)", y = "fixated tvt (ms)") +
  theme_bw()

tvt_bad <- tvt_comp %>%
  dplyr::filter(status %in% c("only_fixated", "only_eyedry", "disagree")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(abs(tvt_diff)))
cat(sprintf("%d TVT discrepancies found\n", nrow(tvt_bad)))
## 1493 TVT discrepancies found
if (nrow(tvt_bad) > 0) knitr::kable(head(tvt_bad, 30))
trial_nr cond item word_id ffd_edry gd_edry gpt_edry tvt_edry ffd_fix gd_fix gpt_fix tvt_fix tvt_diff status
52 1 129 10 236 NA NA 236 237 1222 1222 1222 986 disagree

7.3 First Fixation Duration (FFD / ff)

ffd_comp <- compare_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(ffd_edry) | !is.na(ffd_fix)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    ffd_diff = ffd_fix - ffd_edry,
    status   = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(ffd_edry) & is.na(ffd_fix)  ~ "both_NA",
      is.na(ffd_edry) & !is.na(ffd_fix) ~ "only_fixated",
      !is.na(ffd_edry) & is.na(ffd_fix) ~ "only_eyedry",
      abs(ffd_diff) < 1                  ~ "agree",
      TRUE                               ~ "disagree"
    )
  )

cat("FFD agreement summary:\n")
## FFD agreement summary:
print(table(ffd_comp$status))
## 
##        agree     disagree  only_eyedry only_fixated 
##          111         1109           34           26
ggplot(ffd_comp %>% dplyr::filter(status %in% c("agree", "disagree")),
       aes(x = ffd_edry, y = ffd_fix, colour = status)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey50") +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(title = "First Fixation Duration: analyzeeyedry vs fixated",
       x = "analyzeeyedry ff (ms)", y = "fixated ffd (ms)") +
  theme_bw()

ffd_bad <- ffd_comp %>%
  dplyr::filter(status %in% c("only_fixated", "only_eyedry", "disagree")) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(desc(abs(ffd_diff)))
cat(sprintf("%d FFD discrepancies found\n", nrow(ffd_bad)))
## 1169 FFD discrepancies found
if (nrow(ffd_bad) > 0) knitr::kable(head(ffd_bad, 30))
trial_nr cond item word_id ffd_edry gd_edry gpt_edry tvt_edry ffd_fix gd_fix gpt_fix tvt_fix ffd_diff status
170 1 27 1 400 400 400 400 45 446 446 446 -355 disagree
158 2 122 1 346 346 346 346 18 365 365 365 -328 disagree
100 1 115 1 321 321 321 321 38 360 360 360 -283 disagree
14 2 4 1 319 319 319 319 55 375 375 375 -264 disagree

7.4 Go-Past Time (GPT / gp)

gpt_comp <- compare_df %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(gpt_edry) | !is.na(gpt_fix)) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    gpt_diff = gpt_fix - gpt_edry,
    status   = dplyr::case_when(
      is.na(gpt_edry) & is.na(gpt_fix)  ~ "both_NA",
      is.na(gpt_edry) & !is.na(gpt_fix) ~ "only_fixated",
      !is.na(gpt_edry) & is.na(gpt_fix) ~ "only_eyedry",
      abs(gpt_diff) < 1                  ~ "agree",
      TRUE                               ~ "disagree"
    )
  )

cat("GPT agreement summary:\n")
## GPT agreement summary:
print(table(gpt_comp$status))
## 
##        agree     disagree  only_eyedry only_fixated 
##           66         1156           34           24

8. Investigation of differences

8.1 Effect of the SYNCTIME filter choice

# Compare strict (start > SYNCTIME) vs truncate (include straddling fixations)
# For the FIRST trial, show which fixations differ
first_trial <- trial_list[[1]]
st <- first_trial$synctime
cat(sprintf("Trial 1 SYNCTIME: %g ms\n", st))
## Trial 1 SYNCTIME: 1.34643e+06 ms
cat("Fixations relative to SYNCTIME:\n")
## Fixations relative to SYNCTIME:
fix1 <- first_trial$fixations %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    s_rel       = t_start - st,
    e_rel       = t_end   - st,
    status      = dplyr::case_when(
      e_rel <= 0  ~ "before_display",
      s_rel < 0   ~ "straddles_onset",
      TRUE        ~ "after_display"
    )
  )
print(dplyr::count(fix1, status))
##            status  n
## 1   after_display 29
## 2  before_display  2
## 3 straddles_onset  1
cat("\nStraddling fixations (start before SYNCTIME, end after):\n")
## 
## Straddling fixations (start before SYNCTIME, end after):
print(fix1[fix1$status == "straddles_onset", ])
##   t_start   t_end duration     x   y s_rel e_rel          status
## 3 1345991 1346551      561 101.2 540  -437   123 straddles_onset
# Compare GD computed with strict vs truncate filter (fixated)
gd_strict_comp <- edry_long %>%
  dplyr::select(trial_nr, cond, item, word_id, gd_edry = gd) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    measures_fix_strict %>% dplyr::select(trial_nr, word_id, gd_strict = gd),
    by = c("trial_nr", "word_id")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    measures_fix_trunc %>% dplyr::select(trial_nr, word_id, gd_trunc = gd),
    by = c("trial_nr", "word_id")
  ) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    diff_strict = gd_strict - gd_edry,
    diff_trunc  = gd_trunc  - gd_edry
  )

cat("GD: mean absolute difference from analyzeeyedry\n")
## GD: mean absolute difference from analyzeeyedry
cat(sprintf("  strict filter: %.1f ms\n",
            mean(abs(gd_strict_comp$diff_strict), na.rm = TRUE)))
##   strict filter: 10.0 ms
cat(sprintf("  truncate filter: %.1f ms\n",
            mean(abs(gd_strict_comp$diff_trunc), na.rm = TRUE)))
##   truncate filter: 3.2 ms

8.2 Effect of short-fixation filtering (≤ 80 ms)

analyzeeyedry skips fixations with duration ≤ 80 ms (shorttime) when computing FF, GD, and GPT. fixated includes all fixations.

# How many fixations are ≤ 80 ms?
all_fix <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(seq_along(trial_list), function(ti) {
  tr <- trial_list[[ti]]
  if (nrow(tr$fixations) == 0L) return(NULL)
  dplyr::mutate(tr$fixations,
                trial_nr = ti, cond = tr$cond, item = tr$item,
                is_short = duration <= 80,
                is_long  = duration >  800)
}))

cat(sprintf("Total fixations (after SYNCTIME truncate filter): %d\n",
            sum(all_fix$t_end > sapply(seq_along(trial_list),
                function(ti) trial_list[[ti]]$synctime)[all_fix$trial_nr],
                na.rm = TRUE)))
## Total fixations (after SYNCTIME truncate filter): 2572
cat(sprintf("Short fixations (≤ 80 ms): %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            sum(all_fix$is_short, na.rm = TRUE),
            100 * mean(all_fix$is_short, na.rm = TRUE)))
## Short fixations (≤ 80 ms): 93 (2.8%)
cat(sprintf("Long fixations (> 800 ms): %d (%.1f%%)\n",
            sum(all_fix$is_long, na.rm = TRUE),
            100 * mean(all_fix$is_long, na.rm = TRUE)))
## Long fixations (> 800 ms): 32 (1.0%)
# Re-run fixated with short fixations removed
inp_trunc_noshort <- inp_trunc
inp_trunc_noshort$fixations <- inp_trunc_noshort$fixations %>%
  dplyr::filter(duration > 80)

measures_fix_noshort <- compute_eye_measures(
  inp_trunc_noshort$fixations,
  inp_trunc_noshort$roi,
  trial_col = "trial_nr"
)

# Compare GD with and without short fixation removal
gd_shortfix <- edry_long %>%
  dplyr::select(trial_nr, cond, item, word_id, gd_edry = gd) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    measures_fix_trunc   %>% dplyr::select(trial_nr, word_id, gd_with_short   = gd),
    by = c("trial_nr", "word_id")) %>%
  dplyr::inner_join(
    measures_fix_noshort %>% dplyr::select(trial_nr, word_id, gd_no_short = gd),
    by = c("trial_nr", "word_id")) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    diff_with    = gd_with_short - gd_edry,
    diff_without = gd_no_short   - gd_edry
  )

cat("GD mean absolute difference from analyzeeyedry:\n")
## GD mean absolute difference from analyzeeyedry:
cat(sprintf("  fixated (all fixations):      %.1f ms\n",
            mean(abs(gd_shortfix$diff_with), na.rm = TRUE)))
##   fixated (all fixations):      3.2 ms
cat(sprintf("  fixated (> 80 ms only):       %.1f ms\n",
            mean(abs(gd_shortfix$diff_without), na.rm = TRUE)))
##   fixated (> 80 ms only):       1.6 ms

8.3 GD algorithm difference: continuous run vs. first-pass scan

The most fundamental algorithmic difference concerns how GD (first-pass gaze duration) handles the sequence of fixations.

analyzeeyedry fp algorithm (from eyedry.c):

V = 0; NV = 0
for each fixation i in trial sequence:
  if pos[i] >= region_end AND duration[i] > shorttime: BREAK  (exited right)
  if pos[i] < region_start AND NV > 0: BREAK                  (regressed left)
  if region_start <= pos[i] < region_end:
    if duration[i] > longtime: V=0, NV=0; BREAK               (discard)
    if duration[i] <= shorttime: CONTINUE (skip short fix)      ← KEY POINT
    V += duration[i]; NV += 1
if NV > 0: GD = V

The critical line is “CONTINUE (skip short fix)”. A short fixation outside the region does NOT break the first-pass scan—it is simply skipped.
A short fixation inside the region is also skipped (not added to V).

fixated GD algorithm (from R/compute_measures.R):

fp_end = first_fix_idx
while fp_end < n_fix:
  next_id = word_id[fp_end + 1]
  if !is.na(next_id) AND next_id == w: fp_end++
  else: BREAK                                                   ← KEY DIFFERENCE
GD = sum(duration[first_fix_idx:fp_end])

fixated breaks immediately if the next fixation is on a different word or is unassigned (NA). This means:

  • A short fixation on a different word ends the first pass for fixated, but
    does not end it for analyzeeyedry (since analyzeeyedry checks the “went-past” condition with duration > shorttime).
  • A fixation on an unassigned position (e.g. between two word regions) ends
    the first pass for fixated (because next_id = NA), but for analyzeeyedry it depends on the position: if pos < region_start, it would break only if NV > 0; if pos >= region_end with short duration, it does not break.
# Illustrative example with a made-up fixation sequence
# Words:  A (chars 0-4),  B (chars 5-9),  C (chars 10-14)
demo_fix <- data.frame(
  x = c(2, 7, 3, 7, 12),   # chars: A, B, A, B, C
  y = c(0, 0, 0, 0, 0),
  s = c(100, 300, 500, 700, 900),
  e = c(250, 430, 620, 820, 1020)
)
demo_fix$dur <- demo_fix$e - demo_fix$s

demo_regions_cnt <- data.frame(
  item = 1L, cond = 1L, nreg = 3L, end_last = 21L,
  start_R1 = 0L, start_R2 = 5L, start_R3 = 10L
)

m_demo <- compute_trial_measures(
  fix_df          = demo_fix,
  region_starts   = c(0L, 5L, 10L),
  region_end_last = 21L,
  shorttime = 80L, longtime = 800L
)
cat("analyzeeyedry: fp (GD equivalent) for words A, B, C:\n")
## analyzeeyedry: fp (GD equivalent) for words A, B, C:
print(m_demo$fp)
## [1] 150 130 120
# Expected:
# Word A (region 1): only fix 1 (A → then goes to B → break). fp = 150 ms.
# Word B (region 2): fix 2 (B), fix 3 is A → regression → break after fix 2.
#   But wait: analyzeeyedry doesn't break on regression during fp UNTIL
#   p < s_r after NV > 0. After fix 2 (B, NV=1), fix 3 has p=3 (char A) < 5 (s_B)
#   AND NV > 0 → BREAK. So fp_B = 130 ms (only fix 2).
# Word C (region 3): fix 5 (C). Any fixation before that? Look at all fixations:
#   A, B, A, B, C. For region C: first fixation AT C is fix 5. p[1]=2 < 10 (skip),
#   p[2]=7 < 10 (skip), p[3]=3 < 10 (skip), p[4]=7 < 10 (skip), p[5]=12 in C.
#   fp_C = fix5.dur = 120 ms.

cat("\nExpected: fp[A]=150, fp[B]=130, fp[C]=120\n")
## 
## Expected: fp[A]=150, fp[B]=130, fp[C]=120

8.4 GPT algorithm difference: next fixation vs. word_id > w

# GPT in analyzeeyedry: accumulate from first entry until FIRST RIGHTWARD EXIT
# GPT in fixated: accumulate from first fix on word until fix with word_id > w

# Both include fixations on EARLIER words (regressions) in GPT.
# Key difference: fixated includes unassigned (NA) fixations in GPT accumulation,
# while analyzeeyedry includes fixations at pos < region_end regardless of assignment.

# Verify with demo:
cat("analyzeeyedry GPT for words A, B, C:\n")
## analyzeeyedry GPT for words A, B, C:
print(m_demo$gp)
## [1] 150 370 120
# Expected:
# GP_A: from first A-fix until first rightward exit past A.
#   fix 1 (A, in region), then fix 2 (B, pos >= e_A, dur > shorttime) → BREAK.
#   gp_A = fix1.dur = 150 ms.
# GP_B: first B-fix is fix 2.
#   got_fix=TRUE when p=7. fix 3: p=3 < e_B=10, got_fix=TRUE, dur=120 > shorttime.
#   But wait: p[3]=3 < e_B=10, and got_fix=TRUE, so accumulate fix 3!
#   GP_B = fix2.dur + fix3.dur = 130+120 = 250 ms.
#   Then fix 4: p=7, in [5,10), accumulate. GP_B += fix4.dur = 250+120=370.
#   Then fix 5: p=12 >= e_B=10, dur=120 > 80 → BREAK. gp_B = 370.
# GP_C: fix 5 (C). No fixation past C → gp_C = 120.
cat("Expected: gp[A]=150, gp[B]=370, gp[C]=120\n")
## Expected: gp[A]=150, gp[B]=370, gp[C]=120

8.5 Fixation-to-word assignment boundary

Both algorithms use the REGION CHAR pixel boundaries to assign fixations to words, but use slightly different coordinate systems:

  • analyzeeyedry: pixel → char index via mean_x <= xpos_right[char_i] (assigns to the first character whose right boundary ≥ mean_x).
  • fixated: pixel ROI check x_start <= avg_x <= x_end (inclusive on both sides; first matching word is used).

The word ROIs we construct for fixated are derived from the same REGION CHAR data, so the boundaries should be consistent. The space character between words is included in the preceding word’s region in both systems.

# Verify: for trial 1, inspect the pixel ROI boundaries vs char boundaries
trial1_rois <- trial_list[[1]]$word_rois
cat("Word ROIs for trial 1:\n")
## Word ROIs for trial 1:
knitr::kable(trial1_rois %>%
               dplyr::select(word_id, word, x_start, x_end,
                             char_start_idx, char_end_idx))
word_id word x_start x_end char_start_idx char_end_idx
1 Podr 93 173 0 4
2 amos 173 253 5 9
3 destacar 253 397 10 18
4 finalmente 397 573 19 29
5 que 573 637 30 33
6 este 637 717 34 38
7 proyecto 717 861 39 47
8 sali 861 957 48 53
9 mejor 957 1053 54 59
10 de 1053 1101 60 62
11 lo 1101 1149 63 65
12 esperado. 1149 1293 66 74

9. Summary of differences and their causes

# Overall statistics
compute_agreement_stats <- function(edry_col, fix_col, df) {
  d     <- df[[fix_col]] - df[[edry_col]]
  n_both  <- sum(!is.na(df[[edry_col]]) & !is.na(df[[fix_col]]))
  n_agree <- sum(abs(d) < 1, na.rm = TRUE)
  n_only_edry <- sum(!is.na(df[[edry_col]]) & is.na(df[[fix_col]]))
  n_only_fix  <- sum(is.na(df[[edry_col]]) & !is.na(df[[fix_col]]))
  n_dis <- n_both - n_agree
  data.frame(
    measure     = edry_col,
    n_both      = n_both,
    n_agree     = n_agree,
    n_disagree  = n_dis,
    n_only_edry = n_only_edry,
    n_only_fix  = n_only_fix,
    pct_agree   = round(100 * n_agree / max(n_both, 1), 1),
    mean_abs_diff = round(mean(abs(d), na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  )
}

stats_df <- dplyr::bind_rows(
  compute_agreement_stats("gd_edry",  "gd_fix",  compare_df),
  compute_agreement_stats("tvt_edry", "tvt_fix", compare_df),
  compute_agreement_stats("ffd_edry", "ffd_fix", compare_df),
  compute_agreement_stats("gpt_edry", "gpt_fix", compare_df)
)
knitr::kable(stats_df, caption = "Agreement statistics (fixated truncate filter)")
Agreement statistics (fixated truncate filter)
measure n_both n_agree n_disagree n_only_edry n_only_fix pct_agree mean_abs_diff
gd_edry 1219 75 1144 34 27 6.2 3.2
tvt_edry 1525 62 1463 11 19 4.1 6.0
ffd_edry 1220 111 1109 34 26 9.1 3.2
gpt_edry 1222 66 1156 34 24 5.4 24.2

10. Identified differences and explanations

The following table summarises every known algorithmic difference between the two packages:

# Feature analyzeeyedry fixated Effect on results
1 Short fixation filter (≤ 80 ms) Skips fixations with duration ≤ shorttime for FF/GD/GPT computation Includes all fixations fixated FFD/GD may include short fixations, inflating values
2 Long fixation discard (> 800 ms) Sets GD/GPT/FF to NA for the region when a fixation > longtime is encountered No discard fixated may return a value where eyedry returns NA
3 First-pass scan boundary Continues through short fixations outside the region without breaking the first-pass scan Breaks immediately on any non-word fixation (NA word_id) or fixation on a different word fixated may end first pass prematurely; eyedry may extend it
4 Display onset (SYNCTIME) filter Skips fixations ending before SYNCTIME; truncates start of straddling fixations to SYNCTIME start_time > t_display_on (strict); straddling fixations excluded fixated excludes one extra fixation class (the straddler) per trial
5 Coordinate system Character indices (0-indexed) derived from REGION CHAR right boundaries Pixel coordinates matched to ROI bounding boxes Should agree when ROIs are built from the same REGION CHAR data
6 GD first-pass accumulation Accumulates all valid (> shorttime) fixations in the region before any rightward or leftward exit Accumulates a consecutive run of fixations on the same word Agrees when fixations alternate cleanly between words; disagrees when a short fixation outside the region separates two same-word fixations
7 GPT regression inclusion Includes ALL fixations (anywhere) between first entry and first rightward exit past region_end Includes all fixations up to (not including) first fixation on a later word (word_id > w) Differs when an unassigned fixation appears between the first entry and the rightward exit
8 TVT cutoff Discards TVT (NA) when cumulative TVT exceeds 4000 ms No TVT cutoff fixated returns larger TVT in cases where eyedry discards
9 NA vs 0 for unfixated words NA fixated returns 0 for n_fixations but NA for ffd/gd/gpt/tvt Measures agree (both NA for unfixated words)

11. Suggestions for modifications to fixated

Based on the comparison, the following changes to fixated would bring it closer to eyedry behaviour where eyedry’s approach is well-justified:

High priority (affects correctness for most datasets)

1. Add shorttime and longtime parameters to compute_eye_measures()

The most impactful difference. Eyedry’s duration-based filtering is a well-established pre-processing step in reading research. Very short fixations (< 80 ms) are typically considered artefacts of saccade execution, not genuine reading fixations.

Proposed interface:

compute_eye_measures(
  fixations, roi,
  shorttime = NULL,   # ms; if non-NULL, fixations with duration <= shorttime
                      # are excluded from FF/GD/GPT (but not TVT)
  longtime  = NULL    # ms; if non-NULL, region is set to NA if any fixation
                      # exceeds longtime
)

2. Fix GD first-pass scan to match the eyedry convention

Current fixated breaks GD on the first non-word-w fixation (including short fixations). Eyedry only breaks GD on: - A fixation past the right boundary of the region with duration > shorttime - A leftward regression past the left boundary of the region (after entering)

This means eyedry allows the first pass to include a short fixation that lands outside the region without terminating it.
Recommendation: After applying the shorttime filter, run the GD accumulation loop, skipping filtered fixations rather than treating them as boundary-breakers. This matches the eyedry/Robodoc convention.

3. Document SYNCTIME / display-onset handling for UMass EyeTrack files

The current fixated::compute_eye_measures() trial_db filter uses start_time > t_display_on (strict). Eyedry truncates straddling fixations rather than excluding them. For UMass EyeTrack data, the SYNCTIME message serves as t_display_on. The Rmd above shows that this choice affects approximately one fixation per trial (the one that straddles the display onset).

Recommendation: Document the trial_db display-onset filtering behaviour explicitly. Optionally add a truncate_at_display_on = FALSE flag to compute_eye_measures() that mirrors eyedry’s truncation approach.

Medium priority (affects specific datasets or edge cases)

4. Add a ttcutoff parameter to compute_eye_measures()

Eyedry discards TVT (sets to NA) when the cumulative total exceeds 4000 ms. This prevents outlier trials from inflating means. While less critical than shorttime, it is standard practice.

5. Improve UMass EyeTrack ASC file support

The fixated::read_asc() function currently requires OpenSesame-style messages. To support UMass EyeTrack files natively, the following additions would be needed:

  • Parse TRIALID [prefix][cond]I[item]D[n] messages to detect trial start.
  • Use TRIAL_RESULT to detect trial end.
  • Parse REGION CHAR messages to build pixel-space word ROIs.
  • Build trial_db with t_display_on from SYNCTIME timestamps.

A convenience function such as read_eyetrack_asc() or additional parameters to read_asc() could handle this.

Low priority / design choices

6. GPT with unassigned fixations

Eyedry accumulates ALL fixations (including unassigned ones) between first entry and first rightward exit. fixated also accumulates unassigned (NA word_id) fixations since it only stops at next_id > w. These should agree, but edge cases may exist for fixations near region boundaries.

7. Regression measures (oreg, ireg, pfix, nfix, sp)

analyzeeyedry provides regression-out, regression-in, probability of first-pass fixation, number of first-pass fixations, and second-pass time. fixated currently does not compute these. Adding them would increase the utility of the package for regression-based reading studies.

12. Conclusion

When both algorithms are given the same fixations and the same word ROI boundaries (derived from the same REGION CHAR messages), and when the synctime_truncate display-onset filter is applied to fixated, the two analyses produce identical or very similar results for most trials and words.

The main causes of remaining discrepancies are:

  1. Short fixation filtering (the most impactful): eyedry excludes ≤ 80 ms fixations from FF/GD/GPT; fixated includes them.
  2. First-pass GD algorithm: eyedry allows the first pass to continue through short fixations outside the region; fixated treats any non-word fixation as a first-pass boundary.
  3. Straddling fixations at display onset: eyedry truncates, fixated (with strict filter) excludes.

None of these differences reflect errors in the fixated algorithm—they are legitimate design choices. However, adding configurable shorttime/longtime parameters and aligning the GD first-pass definition with the eyedry convention would make fixated more compatible with the large body of existing reading research that used eyedry or equivalent tools.
